2605.29697.title: Beyond Trajectory Rewards: Step-level Credit Assignment for Agentic Search via Graph Modeling
2605.29697.paper_summary: In Agentic Search, trajectory-level outcome rewards fail to quantify the behavioral contributions of individual steps, while existing step-level reward methods typically rely on costly tree sampling. We view world knowledge as a latent world graph and each IS task as search within a latent task graph, where effective steps should make graph progress toward the answer node. Based on this prior, we propose Graph-Distance Contribution Reward (GDCR), a step-level process reward that scores newly-retrieved and newly-cited entities by their distance to the answer node in a training-time Entity-Relation (ER) graph. We further propose Step Advantage Policy Optimization (SAPO), which converts GDCR into step-level advantages and combines them with trajectory-level outcome advantages. Experiments on four challenging benchmarks validate the effectiveness of our method.
2605.29697.paper_summary_zh: 在代理搜索中，軌跡級的結果獎勵無法量化單個步驟的行為貢獻，而現有的步驟級獎勵方法通常依賴於昂貴的樹採樣。我們將世界知識視為潛在的世界圖，並將每個IS任務視為在潛在的任務圖中進行搜索，其中有效的步驟應該使圖向答案節點進展。基於這一前提，我們提出了圖距貢獻獎勵（GDCR），這是一種步驟級過程獎勵，通過它們與答案節點在訓練時的實體-關係（ER）圖中的距離來對新檢索和新引用的實體進行評分。我們進一步提出了步驟優勢策略優化（SAPO），將GDCR轉換為步驟級優勢，並將其與軌跡級結果優勢相結合。在四個具有挑戰性的基準上的實驗驗證了我們方法的有效性。
2605.29697.author: Yuchen Liu et.al.
2605.29697.authors: Yuchen Liu, Yingjie Feng, Lixiong Qin, Jiasi Chen, Jianing Yu, Sheng Gao, Sheng Yang, Weiran Xu
